subject: Sociology
title: Special Education and Labeling
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Special Education and Labeling.

hook.text: Black boys are three times more likely to be placed in special education. Is the issue their brains or the system?

buildup.text: Special education provides support for learning differences. But placement decisions carry racial and class bias.

discovery.text: Being labeled special needs can help students access resources — or stigmatize them and lower expectations.

twist.text: Wealthier parents fight labels or choose them strategically. Poor families often lack that advocacy power.

climax.text: Special education shows how well-intentioned systems can reproduce inequality through subjective classification.

punchline.text: The label shapes the child's trajectory.

quiz.question: What issue exists with special education placement?
options: Racial and class biases affect who gets labeled
